title: polanisia dodecandra subsp. uniglandulosa
intro: Polanisia dodecandra subsp. uniglandulosa, also known as the tournesol or clacker plant, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids. The medicinal properties of Polanisia dodecandra subsp. uniglandulosa have been reported to provide several health benefits, including relief from fever, pain, and infections, as well as support for the immune system and skin health. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
